mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of month-over-month RSAFS changes falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth is not followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing aggregate demand, triggering risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the t-test of returns during triggered versus neutral periods is insignificant at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising CPI inflation alongside a stagnant or declining federal funds rate produces sharply negative real interest rates, which mechanically inflate equity valuations by collapsing the real discount rate and forcing capital into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the bivariate model underperforms a univariate benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month growth in industrial production signals supply-chain recovery and operating leverage that lifts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days; this predictive power vanishes when labor markets are tight.
failure_condition: During periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, month-over-month INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as the market rapidly prices in labor market strength.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS surprises do not correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one thousand thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer credit, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of retail sales and industrial production confirms a synchronous demand-supply expansion without binding capacity constraints, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns than either indicator in isolation over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime, month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects real demand strength that drives corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days; this relationship weakens when inflation erodes purchasing power.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is below 250, month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
